patient.query: I don’t know, maybe it’s just my own insecurities. I’ve always felt like I had to be perfect or at least good enough. When I’m in a group, it’s like I’m just waiting for someone to point out my flaws or say something that makes me feel small. It’s exhausting.

I am not good enough
someone will point out my flaws
I will feel small

patient.query: I think it started a long time ago, like in school. I always wanted to fit in and be liked, so I thought if I just did everything right, people would accept me. But the more I tried, the more I felt like I was just... pretending. It’s like I could never quite measure up, you know?

patient.query: Yeah, it’s like… I put on this mask, trying to show a version of myself that I think people want to see. But inside, I just feel anxious and disconnected, like I'm not really there. I'm not really there
I need to hide my true self

patient.query: I guess it’s just this belief I've had for so long that if I'm not perfect or lovable in a certain way, then people won't want me around. I don’t know, maybe it’s from things people have said in the past. It’s like I’ve built this idea that I have to earn my place, and if I don’t, then… I’ll be alone.

triggers: things people have said in the past

patient.query: Um, well, when I was younger, I remember some kids teasing me about things like my clothes or how I looked. It was just little comments, but they stuck with me. And then there were times when adults would praise my siblings for their accomplishments, but I felt like I was always just... in the background. It made me think that I had to do something amazing to get that kind of attention, or love, really.

I am not good enough
I am always in the background

patient.query: Yeah, when I catch myself trying to get that attention, it’s like this whirlwind of thoughts. Like, if I don’t do something impressive, then I’ll just be invisible, or worse, people will just think I'm boring. It’s a lot of pressure, and sometimes I feel like I’m on this hamster wheel, just running and running, but I’m not really going anywhere. I just want to feel valued, I guess.

people will think I'm boring

patient.query: I notice that I start to doubt myself a lot. Like, if I don’t get a reaction, I think maybe I’m not good enough or interesting enough. It feels like this heavy weight that comes down on me, making me more anxious. Sometimes I even feel embarrassed for trying so hard, like I should just be able to be myself without all the extra stuff. But then I just freeze up and don’t know what to do.

I'm not interesting enough
I should just be able to be myself

It’s frustrating because I want to connect with people, but then I get all caught up in my head, and that just makes everything worse.

I didn’t meet some invisible standard
